title: Tips-to-Tool Pipeline
subtitle: How community hacks become native Claude Code features
description: Tracks the velocity from community discovery to official implementation. ~4-6 week average.

events.name: Memory / Context Persistence
events.community_signal: Claude-Mem plugin (3,222 likes), Ralph Wiggum auto-restore pattern (3,607 likes)
events.notes: Community built multiple solutions (Claude-Mem, Continuous Claude, QMD). Anthropic shipped a native auto-memory that subsumes most of them.

events.name: Git Worktrees for Parallel Work
events.community_signal: Users sharing manual worktree setups for running parallel Claude instances
events.ship_signal: Native worktree support in Claude Code
events.notes: Community was manually managing git worktrees. Anthropic built it in with automatic branch management.

events.name: Auto-Restore (Ralph Wiggum)
events.community_signal: Geoffrey Huntley's keep-it-simple loop pattern (3,607 likes), community plugin
events.notes: The community version was a plugin/prompt pattern. The native version uses hooks. The core insight (persist state across compaction) is the same but the mechanism is different.

events.name: Skills / Slash Commands
events.ship_signal: Enhanced skills with frontmatter, context:fork, agent teams
events.notes: Skills existed from launch but were basic. Community usage patterns drove the frontmatter system, fork isolation, and hook integration.

events.name: Design-to-Code (DESIGN.md)
events.community_signal: Community experiments with design system files for Claude
events.ship_signal: Google ships DESIGN.md as portable design system spec (2,522 likes)
events.notes: Parallel evolution. Google and community converged on the same idea independently. Not a community-to-tool pipeline but a convergence.
